notes: Landlords must give at least 3 full calendar months' notice (approx. 24 days minimum formal notice).
Annual rent increase is capped by the rate set by the BC government.
Some properties are exempt from the cap.

citations.statute: Residential Tenancy Act, SBC 2002, c 78, s 42(1)
citations.excerpt: A landlord may increase the rent only if the landlord has given the tenant at least 3 months' notice.

citations.statute: Residential Tenancy Regulation, BC Reg 477/2003, s 7
citations.excerpt: The maximum annual rent increase is set by regulation.

notes: Placeholder cap of 3.5%. Verify current year's allowed increase with RTB.
